subject: Cloud Computing
title: Backup and Disaster Recovery for Data
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Backup and Disaster Recovery for Data.

hook.text: GitLab lost six hours of production data in 2017. Five backup methods failed. Only one partial backup worked.

buildup.text: Cloud backups should follow the 3-2-1 rule: three copies, on two different media, with one copy offsite.

discovery.text: Cross-region backups protect against regional outages. Cross-account backups protect against account compromise.

twist.text: Backups are worthless if never tested. Many teams discover broken backups only during an actual disaster.

climax.text: Cloud makes backups easy to automate. But automation without verification is just a false sense of security.

punchline.text: Backup is not the goal. Successful restore is the goal.

quiz.question: What is the 3-2-1 backup rule?
options: Three copies, on two different media types, with one copy stored offsite
Back up three times a day, twice a week, once a month
Three servers, two regions, one cloud provider
